metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: Streamed response ended without content or tool calls

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the mcp-publisher version (e.g. `PUBLISHER_VERSION=vX.Y.Z`) in the install step so the workflow is reproducible across runs.
findings.finding: The mcp-publisher install step specifies 'curl the release binary, os/arch-detected' without pinning a specific version or release tag, making the install non-deterministic across runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Set a `PUBLISHER_VERSION` env var (e.g. `v0.1.0`) in the job and reference the canonical upstream install one-liner with that pinned version, e.g. `curl -fsSL https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/releases/download/${PUBLISHER_VERSION}/mcp-publisher-linux-amd64 -o mcp-publisher`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the `mcp-publisher` binary to a specific version (e.g., `MPPUB_VERSION=vX.Y.Z`) in the curl install step so upgrades are explicit and the workflow is reproducible.
findings.finding: The plan describes fetching the `mcp-publisher` binary via curl with os/arch detection but never specifies pinning to a particular version. A floating/unversioned binary fetch means a breaking upstream release silently breaks the publish step with no reproducible fallback.
findings.location: Implementation section — 'install mcp-publisher (curl the release binary, os/arch-detected)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pinned version variable at the top of the install step, e.g., `MPPUB_VERSION=v0.1.0` (or latest stable at merge time), and include it in the curl URL. Document the version in the step or a comment so future maintainers know where to bump it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (in the plan or in `docs/releasing.md`) whether `mcp-publisher publish` is idempotent for an already-published version, and add a note on the recovery path if the job must be re-run on the same tag.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether `mcp-publisher publish` is idempotent on re-run (e.g., if the CI job fails and is manually re-triggered on the same tag, or if the same tag is pushed twice). If the registry rejects a duplicate version with a hard error, operators have no documented recovery path.
findings.location: Implementation section — Steps / `mcp-publisher publish`; Acceptance Criteria line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the Implementation section: 'If the job is re-run on the same tag, `mcp-publisher publish` is expected to [no-op / error with exit 0 / require manual skip] — confirm this against the upstream mcp-publisher docs and document the operator recovery step in `docs/releasing.md`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a checksum verification step after downloading the mcp-publisher binary (e.g., `sha256sum --check` against a pinned hash from the upstream release) before executing it.
findings.finding: The plan describes installing mcp-publisher by curling a release binary with no integrity verification (no checksum or signature check), making the CI job vulnerable to a compromised or substituted binary silently publishing a malformed or malicious `server.json` to the MCP Registry.
findings.location: Implementation section — 'install mcp-publisher (curl the release binary, os/arch-detected)'
findings.suggested_fix: Pin the mcp-publisher version and add: `echo '<expected-sha256>  mcp-publisher' | sha256sum --check` immediately after the curl download step in the workflow YAML.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` is a real, shipped subcommand by consulting the mcp-publisher release notes or running `mcp-publisher --help` / `mcp-publisher login --help`, and document the confirmed syntax (or corrected command) in the plan before merge.
findings.finding: The plan's central CLI subcommand `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` is unverifiable from the codebase: it appears nowhere in any repo file. The only evidenced auth subcommand is `mcp-publisher login github` (browser/device flow), documented in docs/releasing.md lines 133-137. The plan cites an external URL (modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions) as the source, but that URL is not reachable for verification and no in-repo evidence (docs, scripts, CI configs) corroborates that `github-oidc` is a shipped subcommand of mcp-publisher.
findings.location: Implementation section — Steps: `./mcp-publisher login github-oidc`; also AC line 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the `docs/releasing.md` edit target to also update the `## Accounts / prerequisites` stanza (line 27-28) so it reflects that MCP Registry publishing now uses OIDC in CI rather than the manual `mcp-publisher login github` device flow.
findings.finding: The plan scopes `docs/releasing.md` edits to 'section 4' (### 4. Update the MCP Registry), but the `## Accounts / prerequisites` section (line 27-28) also describes the manual device-flow as the current auth method ('MCP Registry publishing authenticates via `mcp-publisher login github`'). This stanza is not named as an edit target, so after the change it will still describe the interactive device-flow as the live mechanism — contradicting the automation the plan introduces.
findings.location: docs/releasing.md — `## Accounts / prerequisites` section (lines 27-28); Plan section: Implementation / 'Update docs/releasing.md section 4'
findings.suggested_fix: In `## Accounts / prerequisites`, replace the MCP Registry bullet with something like: '- MCP Registry publishing is now automated via OIDC in `.github/workflows/release.yml` (no stored secret, no manual login); the manual `mcp-publisher login github` device flow is retained only as a fallback.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (by reading the MCP Registry's GitHub Actions OIDC docs at `modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions`) exactly how the `io.github.navapbc/*` namespace is authorized under OIDC — specifically whether `navapbc` org membership must still be public or whether the repo's OIDC `sub` claim (`repo:navapbc/rebar:...`) is sufficient — and document that prereq in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that a GitHub Actions OIDC token for `navapbc/rebar` automatically authorizes publishing under the `io.github.navapbc/*` namespace, but the existing codebase documentation records a different authorization mechanism for that same namespace: `docs/releasing.md §4` requires a human user's navapbc org membership to be **public** at login time. It is unspecified whether the MCP Registry's OIDC path uses repo-identity claims (e.g. `sub: repo:navapbc/rebar:...`) to authorize the org namespace, or whether it still requires separate proof of org membership. If the Registry's OIDC path checks org claims differently from the device-flow path, the job will succeed the `login github-oidc` step but fail `publish` with a 403.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact CLI spelling of the OIDC login subcommand from the canonical `modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions` docs (e.g., is it `login github-oidc`, `login --oidc`, or automatic CI detection?), and update the Implementation and AC steps accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan's central CLI command `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` does not appear anywhere in the codebase. The only documented and evidenced subcommand is `mcp-publisher login github` (device flow). The plan cites `modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions` as the canonical source for `login github-oidc`, but that URL is not reachable from the repo. If this subcommand does not exist (e.g., the actual flag is `--oidc`, or a `ACTIONS_ID_TOKEN_REQUEST_URL` env var is consumed automatically, or the binary handles CI detection without a separate subcommand), the mechanism fails at the login step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` is a shipped subcommand (e.g., by checking the mcp-publisher GitHub releases page or `mcp-publisher --help` output) and cite the exact upstream docs URL or CLI version that confirms it before merging.
findings.finding: The plan's central command `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` is an unverified capability assertion. Every codebase reference to mcp-publisher only evidences `mcp-publisher login github` (device-flow). The plan cites `modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions` as the canonical source, but this URL does not appear in the codebase and cannot be confirmed. If `github-oidc` is not a shipped subcommand, the entire automation fails silently or with a confusing error at release time.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a plan note pinning the mcp-publisher version and confirming the `github-oidc` subcommand exists in that version's `--help` output. If the subcommand is `mcp-publisher login --oidc` or similar, update the Implementation and AC accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (via the mcp-publisher docs or upstream GitHub Actions recipe) that a GitHub Actions OIDC token for `navapbc/rebar` is sufficient to publish under `io.github.navapbc/rebar` without requiring a human user's public org membership claim, and document this in the plan or in `docs/releasing.md §4`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that a GitHub Actions OIDC token for the `navapbc/rebar` repo automatically authorizes publishing under the `io.github.navapbc/*` org namespace on the MCP Registry. This is an unverified capability claim. The existing `docs/releasing.md §4` explicitly states that org-namespace publishing requires the human user's navapbc org membership to be public ('captured at login time'). A GitHub Actions OIDC token asserts repo identity (`navapbc/rebar`), not a human user's org membership — it is not established whether the MCP Registry's OIDC path maps a repo token to org-namespace authorization.
findings.location: Why section — 'the runner's OIDC token proves the `navapbc/rebar` repo identity, which authorizes the `io.github.navapbc/*` namespace'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` exists as a shipped subcommand (e.g., via `mcp-publisher --help` / `mcp-publisher login --help` on the current release binary, or by citing the exact upstream docs/changelog URL) and record the result — MATCH or MISMATCH — before merging.
findings.finding: The plan's central CLI subcommand `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` is UNVERIFIED: it does not appear anywhere in the codebase or local docs — only `mcp-publisher login github` (device/browser flow) is evidenced. The plan cites `modelcontextprotocol.io/registry/github-actions` as the source but that URL is external and uncacheable at review time. If the subcommand does not exist in the current mcp-publisher release, the entire job fails silently at login.
findings.location: Implementation section — Steps bullet; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: Before merge, install the current mcp-publisher release binary locally and run `./mcp-publisher login --help` to confirm `github-oidc` is a listed subcommand. Paste the relevant --help output into the plan or a PR comment as evidence. If the subcommand is absent, open a SPIKE to track when upstream adds it, and keep the manual login path as primary until then.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (via MCP Registry docs or a test publish) that a GitHub Actions OIDC token from `navapbc/rebar` is sufficient to authorize publishing to the `io.github.navapbc/rebar` entry — documenting any required one-time registry-side configuration (analogous to PyPI's pending publisher registration) in `docs/releasing.md` §4 prerequisites.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that a GitHub Actions OIDC token from the `navapbc/rebar` repo automatically authorizes the `io.github.navapbc/*` org-level namespace on the MCP Registry, but the existing `docs/releasing.md` §4 documents that org-namespace access requires a *human* GitHub identity with public navapbc org membership — a trust model that does not straightforwardly map to a repo-scoped OIDC token. It is unclear whether the MCP Registry's OIDC authorization grants org-namespace claims or only repo-owner-namespace claims (i.e., `io.github.navapbc/rebar` via repo identity vs. the org namespace `io.github.navapbc/*`).
findings.location: Why section; Implementation section — permissions bullet; docs/releasing.md §4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the mcp-publisher install step to a specific release version (e.g., `VERSION=vX.Y.Z` in the curl URL) so the verified CLI interface is stable across runs.
findings.finding: The install step fetches the mcp-publisher binary via `curl` with no version pin ('curl the release binary, os/arch-detected'), making the CLI interface floating. Combined with the already-unverified `login github-oidc` subcommand, a future mcp-publisher release that renames or removes the subcommand will silently break the job with no indication of which version was running.
findings.location: Implementation section — Steps bullet ('install mcp-publisher (curl the release binary, os/arch-detected)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the workflow step, set `MCP_PUBLISHER_VERSION=vX.Y.Z` (the version you verified `login github-oidc` against) and embed it in the curl URL, e.g.: `curl -fsSL https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/mcp-publisher/releases/download/${MCP_PUBLISHER_VERSION}/mcp-publisher-linux-amd64 -o mcp-publisher`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a binary integrity verification step (e.g., download and verify a published SHA-256 checksum or cosign signature for the `mcp-publisher` release binary) before executing it in the `mcp_registry` job — or pin to an immutable release digest — to prevent a compromised artifact from exfiltrating the OIDC `id-token`.
findings.finding: The plan installs `mcp-publisher` via a `curl`-fetched release binary but does not specify integrity verification (checksum or signature check) before executing the binary. This binary subsequently receives and uses the short-lived GitHub Actions OIDC `id-token` to authenticate to the MCP Registry. A compromised or MITM-substituted binary on this path could exfiltrate the OIDC token or publish malicious metadata to the registry under the `io.github.navapbc/*` namespace.
findings.location: Implementation section — 'install mcp-publisher (curl the release binary, os/arch-detected)' step; AC line 2
findings.suggested_fix: After the curl download step, add: `echo "<expected-sha256>  mcp-publisher" | sha256sum -c` using the checksum published alongside the official release. Alternatively, if the upstream project supports it, pin the download URL to an immutable content-addressed artifact (e.g., a GitHub release asset URL locked to a specific tag commit SHA) and document the pin-update procedure in `docs/releasing.md`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
